constraints.text: Default/root sessions are excluded from this project compilation even if raw files exist locally.
ehrlab dirtycsv project sessions must remain separate from harness-self-governance project memory, except for the single export_sanitized boundary seed.

current_problems: Dashboard controls previously exposed too many node-type chips in one unreadable block.
Summary nodes must become high-quality agent context entry points rather than shallow labels.
Plan state must be represented as graph nodes and remain updateable by agent /plan workflows.

decisions.text: All general-profile sessions in the selected corpus are treated as harness-self-governance project history.
Only the ehrlab export_sanitized boundary session is imported into this project compilation; other ehrlab sessions belong to the separate ehrlab dirtycsv project.
v1.0, v1.1, and v2.0 remain one project lineage but must stay phase-separated in the compiled reference.
A human-readable project book should accompany compiled-session artifacts so the archive can serve as a reusable project handbook.

hard_constraints.text: Default/root sessions are excluded from this project compilation even if raw files exist locally.
ehrlab dirtycsv project sessions must remain separate from harness-self-governance project memory, except for the single export_sanitized boundary seed.

key_decisions: All general-profile sessions in scope belong to harness-self-governance.
ehrlab dirtycsv remains a separate project; only export_sanitized boundary seed crosses into harness-self-governance.
Compiled project memory is the default retrieval layer; live session is temporary RAM.
New information must enter pending update / compiled candidate and pass archive gate before becoming durable compiled memory.

key_skills.name: graph-harness-maintain
key_skills.role: Govern project memory, skill/tool nodes, profile boundaries, graph traversal policy, and archive/rehydration safety for this self-governance project.

key_skills.name: hermes-agent
key_skills.role: Configure or integrate Hermes runtime/profile adapters after graph memory protocol is verified.

memory_lifecycle.archive_gate: quality/scope/boundary gate before stable memory mutation
memory_lifecycle.compiled_memory: agent-readable summary/plan/graph artifacts provide long-term project context; they do not override current-turn raw intent
memory_lifecycle.live_session_ram: current active Hermes session raw messages remain live short-term context and are not replaced by compiled graph memory
memory_lifecycle.pending_update: new user facts/decisions/corrections enter here first

miss_policy.current_session_context: preserve_active_session_raw_messages; graph retriever governs historical/compiled project memory only

phase_boundaries.meaning: Establish safe general↔ehrlab boundary through one sanitized export seed only.
Read-only artifacts, provenance, safety boundary, and validation baseline.
Proposal/review/apply-gate model without destructive autonomous execution.
Project-first dashboard, session compilation, context router, summary/plan nodes, filterable skills/tools, and long-term project memory.

project_goal: Make harness-self-governance the stable long-term context layer for Hermes governance work, while preserving phase boundaries and profile separation.

project_identity.one_line: Manual project compilation defines harness-self-governance scope: include all general sessions, include one ehrlab cross-profile governance boundary seed, exclude default/root sessions, and treat the remaining ehrlab sessions as part of the separate ehrlab dirtycsv project while preserving v1.0 / v1.1 / v2.0 as distinct phases within one project narrative.

completed: Compiled general sessions into harness-self-governance project scope with v1/v1.1/v2 phase boundaries.
Separated ehrlab dirtycsv as standalone project while retaining one sanitized boundary seed.
Built project-first dashboard with profile/project/summary/skill/tool graph nodes and read-only safety UI.
Added visual QA fixes: five semantic colors, compact nodes, no accidental text selection, subtle edge selection.
project_plan.source: agent-authored plan node; should be kept in sync with /plan-style work and dashboard state
todo: Keep summary nodes strong enough to serve as default agent long-term context.
Integrate future /plan command outputs into plan nodes with completed/todo state updates.
Use pending update / compiled candidate / archive gate before promoting new session facts to compiled memory.
When project matching fails, create a context gap or pending update instead of guessing a project assignment.

project_status: active_v2_dashboard_memory_graph_hardening; v1/v1.1/v2 boundaries are compiled; UI and summary quality are being aligned to user feedback.
purpose: Build Hermes graph-harness self-governance: a local, auditable, project-level Memory Graph where agents read compiled summaries/plans/decisions/requirements/constraints/evidence before raw sessions.
read_order: project_summary:general:harness-self-governance — start with project goal, status, problems, phase boundaries, decisions, constraints, evidence, plan.
plan:general:harness-self-governance — inspect completed/todo before continuing implementation.
docs/examples/agent-memory-graph/harness-self-governance/compiled-session-project-scope-and-phase-boundary.json — machine-readable compiled scope and phase boundary.
Raw sessions — forensic fallback only when compiled graph lacks evidence.

requirements.text: The compiled project must expose phase boundaries, source-session scope, and anchor milestones so future retrieval can distinguish v1.0 from v2.0 without splitting the project.
The compiled project must preserve the general↔ehrlab governance boundary as a first-class project concept.

summary: Manual project compilation defines harness-self-governance scope: include all general sessions, include one ehrlab cross-profile governance boundary seed, exclude default/root sessions, and treat the remaining ehrlab sessions as part of the separate ehrlab dirtycsv project while preserving v1.0 / v1.1 / v2.0 as distinct phases within one project narrative.
